directionChange.reason: Evaluate how WindowsUtility Project Memory would change if the work direction moved from legacy-preserving retrofit orientation toward behavior-preserving refactor planning.

preservationPolicyImpact: Parity claims require evidence before refactor scope can be trusted.
Read/write/save/load and hardware communication boundaries stay explicit.
improvementPolicyImpact: Refactor work must be behavior-preserving by default.
Shared library or native interop edits require dedicated evidence plans.
UI modernization and platform migration remain separate direction choices.
sourceAuthorityImpact: Detailed CardPrinterConfig slices still require explicit instruction-pack allowed scope.
Project Memory preview does not authorize source mutation.
taxonomyImpact: No extension kind is approved by this candidate.
execution-flow and forbidden-flow-boundary kinds become more important for refactor safety.
native-interop and hardware-boundary gaps should remain visible until mapped.
viewTreeImpact: behavior-preservation tree may need stronger coverage.
integration-target-context stays future migration context only.

nonAuthorityBoundary: This Project Memory impact report reads a direction-change candidate only. It does not approve a project memory revision, apply taxonomy or view tree changes, mutate graph-source, change traversal, generate selected slices or contracts, satisfy Evidence, prove equivalence, enforce scope, or configure CI.
